title: Fermented Dense Venezuelan Corn Flatbread
description: A hearty, fermented corn flatbread with a tangy hint of Dijon mustard, perfect for a cozy date night and easy to fry.
instructions: In a small bowl, dissolve yeast in 1/4 cup of warm water and let sit for 5 minutes until foamy.
In a large mixing bowl, combine cornmeal, flour, and salt.
Add the yeast mixture, remaining warm water, Dijon mustard, and olive oil to the dry ingredients.
Mix until a sticky dough forms; cover with a damp cloth and let ferment at room temperature for 1 hour, until it expands slightly.
Preheat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and add a splash of olive oil.
Divide the dough into 4 equal portions and shape each into a flat, round disk about 1/2 inch thick.
Place one disk in the skillet, cooking 2–3 minutes per side until golden brown and a firm crust forms; transfer to a plate and cover loosely with foil to keep warm.
Repeat with the remaining dough, adding more oil as needed.
Serve the flatbread warm, optionally with a drizzle of melted butter or a side of salsa for extra flavor.
